title: 7 Herbal Teas For Upset Stomach
intro_desc: Herbal teas are a great way to soothe an upset stomach. They work by calming down the digestive system and reducing inflammation in the stomach and intestines. This is because many herbal teas contain anti-inflammatory compounds that help to reduce swelling and ease stomach cramps. For example, ginger (Zingiber officinale) has natural anti-inflammatory properties that make it effective in alleviating nausea and stomach discomfort. Licorice root (Glycyrrhiza glabra) is another herb that has been used for centuries to calm the stomach and reduce inflammation. Fennel (Foeniculum vulgare) is also a popular herbal tea that can help to ease stomach cramps and bloating. When you drink herbal tea, the active compounds are absorbed into your bloodstream and start working to calm down your digestive system. This can bring a range of benefits, including reduced stomach pain, improved digestion, and a sense of relief from discomfort. As a result, you may find that you're able to enjoy your favorite foods again, and that your overall sense of well-being is improved.
faq_1: Drinking herbal tea may help ease an upset stomach. Some teas, like peppermint and ginger, have natural ingredients that can calm the digestive system. These ingredients can help reduce nausea and discomfort. When you feel a stomach issue coming on, a warm cup of herbal tea might help soothe your stomach and make you feel better.
faq_prevent: Drinking herbal tea may help with an upset stomach. Peppermint tea, for example, can calm the stomach and reduce nausea. Ginger tea is another option, as it has anti-inflammatory properties that can soothe the digestive system. These teas can be a natural way to ease stomach discomfort and promote relaxation.
faq_safe: Herbal teas can be a good choice for an upset stomach, but drinking them every day might not be the best idea. Some herbs can be too strong or interact with other medications, causing problems. It's best to try a small amount first and see how your body reacts, so you can enjoy the benefits without any issues.
faq_timeline: Herbal teas can start to ease upset stomach symptoms within 10 to 30 minutes. This is because they contain active compounds that help calm the digestive system. As you drink more, the effects can last for a few hours, giving you relief from nausea and discomfort.
faq_time: Drinking herbal tea for an upset stomach can be most soothing when done in the morning or early afternoon. These times allow the body to digest the tea's calming effects before bed, when it might interfere with sleep. Morning or afternoon tea can help settle your stomach and get you feeling better for the rest of the day.
faq_interaction: Herbal teas can interact with prescription medications for an upset stomach. For example, peppermint tea can slow down digestion, which might affect how well your stomach medications work. Ginger tea can also thin the blood, which can be a problem if you're taking blood thinners. It's essential to be aware of these potential interactions.
faq_pregnant: When you have an upset stomach during pregnancy, some herbal teas might be okay to try. Peppermint tea is often suggested, as it can help with nausea and discomfort. Ginger tea is also a good option, but make sure to choose a product that's made with real ginger and not added flavorings.
faq_breastfeeding: When you're breastfeeding and have an upset stomach, herbal teas can be a soothing option. Look for teas like peppermint, ginger, and chamomile, which are often considered safe. These teas can help calm your stomach and ease discomfort. However, be cautious of teas that may contain caffeine or other stimulants, which can pass to your baby through breast milk.

remedies.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale teas contains a compound called gingerol, which is responsible for its medicinal properties. Gingerol has anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce inflammation in the stomach and relieve nausea. The compound also has a stimulant effect on digestive enzymes, helping to speed up digestion and alleviate symptoms of an upset stomach. The spicy heat of Zingiber officinale teas is also attributed to gingerol, which helps to increase the production of bile and stimulate stomach contractions. This helps to relieve bloating and discomfort associated with indigestion.
remedies.image_alt: Zingiber officinale herbal teas for upset stomach
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of water and 1/2 teaspoon of dried Zingiber officinale root.
Heat the water in a pot over medium heat until it starts boiling.
Remove the pot from heat and add the dried Zingiber officinale root. Let it steep for 5 minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup using a fine mesh sieve or cheesecloth. Discard the root.
Drink the tea slowly and enjoy. You can add honey or lemon to taste if needed.

remedies.remedy_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra teas contains compounds like glycyrrhizin, flavonoids, and phenolic acids. These bioactive constituents have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that can help soothe the stomach lining. Glycyrrhizin, in particular, has been shown to inhibit the production of stomach acid, which can help alleviate heartburn and indigestion. The flavonoids and phenolic acids in the tea have also been found to have a protective effect on the stomach lining, reducing irritation and inflammation. By reducing stomach acid and protecting the stomach lining, Glycyrrhiza glabra teas can help calm an upset stomach.
remedies.image_alt: Glycyrrhiza glabra herbal teas for upset stomach
remedy_procedure: Gather 2 tablespoons of dried Glycyrrhiza glabra roots and 1 cup of boiling water.
Steep the roots in the boiling water for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the mixture into a cup using a tea strainer or a piece of cheesecloth.
Add honey to taste, if desired. Stir well to combine.
Drink the tea as needed to help soothe an upset stomach.

remedies.remedy_desc: Foeniculum vulgare teas contains a compound called apiol, which helps to soothe the stomach and reduce inflammation. The tea also contains a flavonoid called linarin, which has antioxidant properties that can protect the stomach lining from damage. The volatile oils in Foeniculum vulgare, such as anethole and dill apiole, have a calming effect on the digestive system, reducing spasms and gas. These compounds work together to help alleviate symptoms of an upset stomach, such as bloating and cramps. By reducing inflammation and protecting the stomach lining, Foeniculum vulgare teas can help to restore a healthy balance in the digestive system.
remedies.image_alt: Foeniculum vulgare herbal teas for upset stomach
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh Foeniculum vulgare leaves or 1 teaspoon of dried leaves.
Boil 1 cup of water in a pot.
Add the Foeniculum vulgare leaves to the boiling water.
Steep the leaves for 5-7 minutes, then strain the tea into a cup.
Drink the tea 2-3 times a day for upset stomach relief.

remedies.remedy_desc: Mentha x piperita teas contains essential oils like menthol and menthone, which have natural anti-inflammatory properties. These compounds help to reduce stomach inflammation and ease pain caused by digestive issues. The tea also contains limonene, which has antimicrobial properties that can help to eliminate bacteria that may be contributing to stomach upset. Additionally, menthol's ability to relax the muscles in the digestive tract can help to alleviate cramps and spasms. By reducing inflammation and promoting a healthy balance of gut bacteria, Mentha x piperita teas can help to soothe an upset stomach and promote digestive comfort.
remedies.image_alt: Mentha x piperita herbal teas for upset stomach
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh peppermint leaves (Mentha x piperita) or 2 teaspoons of dried peppermint leaves.
Heat 1 cup of water in a pot. Bring it to a boil, then reduce heat to a simmer.
Add the peppermint leaves to the water. If using fresh leaves, use a spoon to bruise them to release oils.
Steep for 5-7 minutes, or until the tea reaches your desired strength.
Strain the tea into a cup and drink. You can add honey or lemon to taste.

remedies.remedy_desc: Cinchona officinalis teas contains alkaloids like quinine and quinidine. These compounds have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and irritation in the stomach. The alkaloids in Cinchona officinalis also have antispasmodic properties, which can calm down stomach muscles and prevent cramping. Additionally, the tea contains flavonoids and phenolic acids that can help reduce inflammation and protect the stomach lining from damage. Drinking Cinchona officinalis tea may help soothe an upset stomach and promote a sense of calm.
remedies.image_alt: Cinchona officinalis herbal teas for upset stomach
remedy_procedure: Gather 2 teaspoons of dried Cinchona officinalis bark and 1 cup of boiling water.
Steep the Cinchona bark in the boiling water for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the mixture into a cup using a tea strainer or cheesecloth.
Add honey or sugar to taste, if needed.
Drink the tea slowly to help soothe an upset stomach.

remedies.remedy_desc: Taraxacum officinale teas contains a mix of compounds like apigenin, luteolin, and taraxasterol. These bioactive constituents have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help soothe an irritated stomach lining. Taraxasterol, in particular, has been shown to reduce inflammation and protect the stomach from damage. The flavonoids in Taraxacum officinale tea, including apigenin and luteolin, have antioxidant properties that can help calm an upset stomach and promote digestion. By reducing inflammation and promoting digestion, Taraxacum officinale tea can provide relief for stomach discomfort.
remedies.image_alt: Taraxacum officinale herbal teas for upset stomach
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh Taraxacum officinale leaves and flowers.
Chop the leaves and flowers finely and add 1 tablespoon to 1 cup of boiling water.
Reduce heat and let it simmer for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the mixture into a cup and discard the solids.
Drink the tea warm or at room temperature as needed for upset stomach.

remedies.remedy_desc: Aloe barbadensis teas contains a mixture of bioactive constituents, including aloin, aloe-emodin, and acemannan. These compounds have anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe the digestive system and reduce inflammation in the gut. The gel of the aloe plant also contains mucilages, which form a protective barrier on the lining of the stomach, shielding it from irritation and acid reflux. Additionally, aloe barbadensis teas contains antioxidants like vitamins A, C, and E, which can help neutralize free radicals and promote healing in the stomach lining. Drinking aloe barbadensis teas may help calm an upset stomach by reducing inflammation and providing a protective barrier against stomach acid.
remedies.image_alt: Aloe barbadensis herbal teas for upset stomach
remedy_procedure: Cut 2-3 inches of aloe vera leaf from a mature plant, avoiding the yellow sap.
Wash the cut leaf with water and pat it dry with a towel.
Remove the outer skin of the leaf and scoop out the clear gel inside.
Mix 2 tablespoons of the aloe gel with 1 cup of boiling water in a cup.
Let the mixture steep for 5-7 minutes, then strain it and drink the tea.
